title: 'The future of [X]' requires a thesis about the present
summary: AI-generated copy claims to represent 'the future of [category]' without articulating what is wrong with the present or the specific thesis for change.
instruction: When claiming 'the future of [X]', articulate the thesis: what is changing, why the present state is insufficient, and how the product addresses that gap.
rationale: 'The future of work' without a thesis about what is wrong with current work is an interchangeable phrase that could headline any product. A specific thesis ('The future of documentation: AI-generated that stays current with your codebase') gives the reader a concrete claim to evaluate.

violations: 'The future of collaboration' with no thesis about what's wrong with current collaboration.
'Built for the future of design' without describing the future state.
controls: 'The future of documentation: automatically generated docs that stay synced with your code.'
'The future of hiring: skills-based matching that removes resume bias.'
borderline: A well-known futurist speaker or publication using 'the future of [X]' as a category descriptor — the speaker's thesis is the content, not a product claim.

exceptions.description: Conference or publication titles where 'the future of [X]' describes the content area. Futurist analysis where the thesis is the product. Academic forecasting.

detector.signalDescription: Find 'the future of' or 'future of' patterns. Check for thesis statements or specific change descriptions nearby.
limitations: Conference titles and publication names legitimately use 'future of' as a category descriptor.
The thesis may be in a subtitle or hero body text rather than the headline.

scenarios: Conference or event names ('The Future of Web Design 2026').
Academic or journalistic writing about future trends.
A detailed thesis with the 'future of' headline backed by body copy — may not be detectable as having a thesis.
mitigations: Check the 200 characters following 'future of' for thesis indicators (specific change, technology, methodology). Exclude event names and publication titles.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Replace 'The future of [X]' with a specific thesis statement: what is changing, why the present approach is insufficient, and how the product addresses the gap.

fixturePlan.description: A landing page with 'The future of project management' as the headline and no supporting thesis.
Same page with 'The future of project management: AI that anticipates bottlenecks before they happen.'
A conference website titled 'The Future of Design' where the content is the thesis.
